regions.text: Reading and thinking show us how ideas, people, and body organs are all interdependent. (Diagram: Grid comparing organs to social systems like direction, work, supply, and transport).
Through reflection, we know ourselves. Shakespeare wrote that thinking must travel to be 'mirrored' where it can see itself.
Troilus and Cressida (Shakespeare). Some think the word was 'married' instead of 'mirrored'.
organ: body part. interdependent: needing each other. reflection: a mirror image. FUTURE: will reflect. PRESENT: reflect(s). PAST: reflected.
